steps.title: Expand both sides
steps.content:
For infinitely many solutions the equation must be an identity (true for every \(x\)). Expand:
Left: \(a(4x-8)+5a = 4ax - 8a + 5a = 4ax - 3a\)
Right: \(4(ax-2a)-20 = 4ax - 8a - 20\)
The \(4ax\) terms are identical on both sides regardless of \(a\), so the \(x\)-coefficient condition is already met. Equate the constant terms:
$$-3a = -8a - 20 \implies 5a = -20 \implies a = -4$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — −4 only: When \(a = -4\): left \(= -16x + 12\), right \(= -16x + 32 - 20 = -16x + 12\). Identity for all \(x\). ✓ And \(a = -4\) is the unique solution to \(5a = -20\), so no other value works. ✓
  • Choice B — 0 only: When \(a = 0\): left \(= 0\), right \(= -20\). This gives \(0 = -20\), which is never true (no solutions, not infinitely many). ✗
  • Choice C — Any real number: Any \(a \neq -4\) gives \(-3a \neq -8a - 20\), producing a contradiction with no solutions. Not every real number works. ✗
  • Choice D — No real number: \(a = -4\) does produce an identity, so at least one value works. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Exactly one value, \(a = -4\), makes the equation an identity with infinitely many solutions. That matches choice A — A is correct.
